title: Character Evidence — General Rule
text: Except as otherwise provided by statute, any otherwise admissible evidence (including evidence in the form of an opinion, evidence of reputation, and evidence of specific instances of such person's conduct) is admissible to prove a person's character or a trait of his character.

structure.division_title: Evidence Affected or Excluded by Extrinsic Policies
structure.chapter_title: Evidence of Character, Habit, or Custom
